summary.name: Long-only QQQ trend with trailing stop
summary.author: Louis

summary.description_full:
The strategy is a long-only trend participation overlay on US large-cap growth equities, implemented via QQQ, designed to capture persistent upside momentum while capping downside through a systematic trailing exit. It maintains full notional exposure (100 percent of portfolio) whenever eligible, then delegates the exit decision to a 5 percent trailing stop that ratchets up with price and liquidates the entire position when the ETF falls sufficiently from its post-entry peak, targeting environments with sustained upward drift but accepting frequent stop-outs in choppy, mean-reverting regimes.

Decisions are evaluated on hourly bars, but the behavior is effectively state-based: enter (or re-enter) immediately when flat, then hold until the trailing stop triggers. After any stop-driven exit, the strategy enforces a 30 calendar day cooldown before taking a new position, aiming to avoid rapid re-entry during unstable drawdowns and volatility clustering. Risk control is concentrated in the fixed trailing-stop distance, the all-in single-instrument exposure, and the post-exit cooldown; there is no additional diversification, volatility targeting, or signal-based position sizing beyond full allocation.

raw.name: Long-only QQQ trend with trailing stop

author.name: Louis
author.bio: I am Louis and I have a passion for options, trading, and computer science. Algorithmic trading is a great way to combine these things.

raw.description:
The strategy is a long-only trend participation overlay on US large-cap growth equities, implemented via QQQ, designed to capture persistent upside momentum while capping downside through a systematic trailing exit. It maintains full notional exposure (100 percent of portfolio) whenever eligible, then delegates the exit decision to a 5 percent trailing stop that ratchets up with price and liquidates the entire position when the ETF falls sufficiently from its post-entry peak, targeting environments with sustained upward drift but accepting frequent stop-outs in choppy, mean-reverting regimes.

Decisions are evaluated on hourly bars, but the behavior is effectively state-based: enter (or re-enter) immediately when flat, then hold until the trailing stop triggers. After any stop-driven exit, the strategy enforces a 30 calendar day cooldown before taking a new position, aiming to avoid rapid re-entry during unstable drawdowns and volatility clustering. Risk control is concentrated in the fixed trailing-stop distance, the all-in single-instrument exposure, and the post-exit cooldown; there is no additional diversification, volatility targeting, or signal-based position sizing beyond full allocation.
